content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of length NNN.

For the array AAA, a pair (i,j)‚ÄÖ(1‚â§i<j‚â§N)(i, j) \: (1\le i\lt j \le N)(i,j)(1‚â§i<j‚â§N) is said to be good if both (Ai+Aj)(A_i + A_j)(Ai‚Äã+Aj‚Äã) and (Ai‚ãÖAj)(A_i \cdot A_j)(Ai‚Äã‚ãÖAj‚Äã) are prime numbers.

You are given QQQ queries in the format:


LLL RRR KKK: Let BBB denote the subarray A[L,R]A[L, R]A[L,R]. You are allowed to update **at most** KKK elements of the array BBB to any non-negative integers.

Find the **maximum** number of good pairs in the array BBB.
Note that A[L,R]A[L, R]A[L,R] denotes the array [AL,AL+1,‚Ä¶,AR][A_L, A_{L+1}, \ldots, A_R][AL‚Äã,AL+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AR‚Äã].


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the length of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
The third line of each test case contains an integer QQQ - the number of queries.
Each of the next QQQ lines contain three space-separated integers L,RL, RL,R and KKK.


Output Format
For each query, output the **maximum** number of good pairs after making the updates.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3‚ãÖ1053 \cdot 10^53‚ãÖ105.
The sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 3‚ãÖ1053 \cdot 10^53‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

Query 111: B=[1,0]B = [1, 0]B=[1,0]. You are allowed to perform at most 222 updates. You can use 111 update to make B=[1,2]B = [1, 2]B=[1,2]. Now (1,2)(1,2)(1,2) is a good pair since B1+B2=3B_1+B_2=3B1‚Äã+B2‚Äã=3 and B1‚ãÖB2=2B_1\cdot B_2=2B1‚Äã‚ãÖB2‚Äã=2 are both prime.
